title: isar (plugin)

sections.markdown: `isar` is a fast native NoSQL DB for Flutter/Dart: schemas from Dart classes via codegen, indexes, full-text search, links, and reactive queries — all with a compact native binary.

Isar's edge: high write throughput + rich queries without SQL migrations. `writeTxn` batches many puts efficiently. `watchLazy()` / `watch()` streams re-emit on change — perfect for offline-first Flutter UIs.
Writing outside a `writeTxn` (throws) or writing one record per transaction (slow). Batch inside a single writeTxn for bulk operations.
One query uses one index at a time — design indexes to match top queries. On desktop/CI, initialize with the correct native library path. Consider Isar v4 / community forks as the ecosystem evolves.

flashcards.back: Wrap them in a single writeTxn() closure.
watch() / watchLazy() streams re-emit on data changes.
One index used per query — design indexes for top query patterns.

quiz.explain: Design indexes to match top queries.

options: Local mobile-first NoSQL with high write throughput

challenges.text: Index + filter query; observe changes with watch().

interviewQuestions: How do you handle schema changes?
How do you optimize a slow Isar query?
